title: English unit 4
content:
Absolutely 👍
I’ll teach you Letter Writing in simple English step by step so it becomes easy for exam.

📘 LETTER WRITING

Letter writing means writing a message to someone in written form.

It is used for:

  • Personal communication

  • Official work

  • Complaints

  • Requests

  • Invitations

  • Information

There are 2 main types of letters:

  1. Informal Letter

  2. Formal Letter

📘 PART 1: Informal Letter

Informal letter is written to:

  • Friends

  • Parents

  • Cousins

  • Relatives

  • Close people

👉 Tone is friendly and personal.

📌 Format of Informal Letter

text
Your Address
Date

Dear (Name),

Body of letter

Yours lovingly / Yours affectionately / Your friend

Your Name

📌 Example Informal Letter

34 Park Avenue
Mumbai – 400023
24 September 2021

Dear Maria,

How are you? I hope you are fine. I heard you came back from Ethiopia safely. I am very happy.

I want to know about your trip. How was the place? Did you enjoy there? I saw your photos on Instagram. They were beautiful.

Please reply soon. I am excited to hear everything.

Your loving cousin,
Sarah

📘 PART 2: Formal Letter

Formal letter is written to:

  • Principal

  • Editor

  • Manager

  • Officer

  • Company

  • School authority

👉 Tone is polite and professional.

📌 Format of Formal Letter

text
Sender’s Address
Date

Receiver’s Address

Subject: ________

Sir/Madam,

Body of letter

Thank you.

Yours faithfully / Yours sincerely

Your Name

📘 PART 3: Example Formal Letter

To Editor about unhealthy diet ads

Brilliant Vidyalaya
Barra, Kanpur
15/02/2023

The Editor
The DWA
Kanpur

Subject: Harmful diet advertisements

Sir/Madam,

I want to bring your attention to harmful posters near our school. These posters promise quick weight loss.

Students may believe these ads and harm their health.

I suggest starting a “Wholesome Lunch Month” in schools. Students should bring fruits, vegetables, and healthy food. Schools can also guide students about healthy eating and positive body image.

Please publish this issue in your newspaper.

Thank you.

Yours sincerely,
Sunidhi Prakash

📘 PART 4: Difference Between Formal and Informal Letter

Formal LetterInformal Letter
Official usePersonal use
Polite toneFriendly tone
Fixed formatFlexible format
Principal, EditorFriend, cousin

📘 PART 5: Important Parts of Letter

1. Sender’s Address

Your address

2. Date

Date of writing

3. Receiver’s Address

Person you send letter to

4. Subject

Main topic of letter

5. Salutation

Greeting

Examples:

  • Dear Sir

  • Dear Madam

  • Dear Rahul

6. Body

Main message

7. Closing

Examples:

  • Yours sincerely

  • Yours faithfully

  • Yours lovingly

8. Signature

Your name

📘 PART 6: Style and Tone

Style = Way of writing

Use:

  • correct grammar

  • proper words

  • short sentences

Tone = Feeling in writing

Examples:

  • Formal

  • Friendly

  • Polite

  • Serious

📘 PART 7: Tips for Good Letter Writing

✔ Clarity

Write clearly.

✔ Concise

Do not write unnecessary things.

✔ Correct Format

Follow proper format.

✔ Polite Language

Use respectful words.

✔ Proofread

Check spelling mistakes.

📘 PART 8: Easy Sentences for Formal Letter

  • I am writing to inform you...

  • I request you to...

  • Kindly look into the matter.

  • Please take necessary action.

  • Thank you.

📘 PART 9: Easy Sentences for Informal Letter

  • How are you?

  • Hope you are fine.

  • I am happy to hear from you.

  • Please reply soon.

  • Take care.

📘 PART 10: Exam Trick

If question says friend/cousin/father

➡️ Informal letter

If question says principal/editor/manager

➡️ Formal letter

📘 Short Revision

Informal Letter

Friendly letter to known person.

Formal Letter

Official letter to authority.

Main Parts

Address → Date → Greeting → Body → Closing → Name

📘 If you want, I can also teach Notice Writing + Email Writing + Resume Writing in same simple English for exams.
